issues.description: FR-1b spec deviation: determine_trigger skips the judge at round 2 for findings with any High or Critical severity (returns None for round < judge_min_round). This means the judge cannot override-accept a round-2 verdict where the reviewer over-classified severity — partially defeating Problem 1 (triviality override). A round-2 verdict with all medium-severity style findings correctly triggers the judge, but adding a single 'high' finding (even if borderline) disables the judge entirely for that round.
issues.suggestion: Document this as a known deviation in the spec or comments. Consider adding a heuristic: if high-severity count <= 1 and it's the only non-low/medium finding, still invoke the judge. Alternatively, the configurable judge_min_round (defaultable to 1) provides an escape hatch for operators.

issues.description: FR-6b requires a Grafana-friendly metric (tracing span attribute) judge_decision_total{decision=...} to track call volume. The implementation only has an INFO-level log at judge.rs line 355. No span attributes or structured metrics are emitted. The spec notes 'Not blocking; the logs are sufficient for Stage 1' but the requirement is still listed in FR-6b.
issues.suggestion: Add a tracing span attribute to the judge INFO log, e.g., tracing::info!(judge_decision_total = 1, decision = output.decision.as_str(), ...) so it can be scraped by Grafana/Prometheus. This is low-effort and satisfies FR-6b fully.

issues.description: NFR-5 test gap: no integration test covers the harden revise-evaluation max_rounds path (lines 751-870 in driver.rs), where the judge is invoked a second time specifically for the max_rounds trigger after a revise completes. Only the audit-evaluation path is tested. The harden revise-evaluation path has its own judge invocation logic with different issue extraction (finds last audit round, not last revise round), making it a distinct code path worth testing.
issues.suggestion: Add an integration test that sets up a hardening loop at max_rounds with a completed revise stage, verifies the judge is invoked with the MaxRounds trigger at the revise evaluation point, and asserts each decision variant produces the correct state transition.

issues.description: Token budget math is tight but workable. MAX_SPEC_CHARS (6K) + MAX_ROUND_CHARS (4K) + MAX_VERDICT_CHARS (2K) = 12K chars for truncatable fields, plus recurring_findings (10 entries × ~200 chars = 2K), system prompt (~2K), and JSON envelope overhead (~2K) totals ~18K chars (~4.5K tokens). The MAX_INPUT_CHARS=28K progressive truncation guard provides safety, but the budget leaves less headroom than ideal for the 8K token limit from FR-4b. A loop with large recurring findings descriptions and verbose round summaries could approach the limit before progressive truncation kicks in.
issues.suggestion: Consider reducing MAX_SPEC_CHARS to 4K and MAX_ROUND_CHARS to 3K to increase headroom, or add a character limit on individual recurring finding descriptions.

issues.description: The evaluate() method takes 9 parameters with #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]. While a context struct would improve readability, the method is internal and called from only one place (invoke_judge_for_stage in driver.rs). Acceptable for Stage 1.
issues.suggestion: No change needed for Stage 1. Consider bundling parameters into a JudgeEvaluateContext struct if the method gains more callers.
